name: Regent Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank offering personal and business checking, savings, lending, mortgages, and specialized services including agricultural, cannabis, and non-profit banking.
description_long:
Regent Bank positions itself as a foremost provider of community banking services with FDIC insurance backing. The bank operates both personal and business divisions, suggesting a regional or mid-size community bank model. Their website emphasizes faith-based values, community involvement, and specialized banking divisions tailored to specific industries and populations.

The bank offers comprehensive personal banking products including checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, CDs, IRAs, personal credit cards, personal loans, mortgages, and insurance products. On the business side, Regent provides business checking, savings, CDs, credit cards, SBA loans, commercial lending, remote deposit capture, and treasury services covering payables, receivables, cash management, fraud mitigation, and merchant services.

Regent's distinguishing factors include specialized banking divisions for agricultural, cannabis, healthcare, Hispanic, non-profit, and tech sectors—indicating intentional focus on underserved or niche markets. The bank emphasizes community engagement through events like the Non-Profit Leadership Series and Executive Luncheon Series, plus a faith component with Prayer Team, Daily Devotional, and Faith in Business initiatives. This positions them as values-driven beyond typical banking operations.

A honest assessment recognizes that the website provides limited specific product details, rates, fees, or competitive metrics. The heavy faith messaging and community focus may appeal strongly to certain demographics but could be neutral or off-putting to others. Without access to their charter, branch locations, asset size, or regulatory history, the full scope of their regional presence and financial stability cannot be independently verified beyond the FDIC insurance claim.
services: Personal and business CDs and IRAs
Wire transfers and remote deposit capture
Insurance products and private wealth management
Treasury services including cash management and fraud mitigation
Specialized banking for agriculture, cannabis, healthcare, Hispanic, non-profit, and tech sectors
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full faith and credit backing from U.S. Government
Specialized banking divisions for underserved sectors: cannabis, agricultural, healthcare, non-profit, and tech banking
Comprehensive SBA loan offerings and commercial lending for business growth
Integrated insurance products, private wealth services, and mortgage lending under one institution
Mobile and online banking platforms with remote deposit capture for convenience
Community-focused approach with non-profit leadership programs and industry events
Faith-based values integration reflected in prayer team, devotionals, and community initiatives
cons: Website lacks specific interest rates, APRs, fees, or product comparison details for informed decision-making
Limited transparency on branch locations, service areas, or geographic coverage from homepage
No visible customer reviews, ratings, or third-party verification of service quality and reliability
Faith-based marketing and community focus may not align with all consumer preferences or expectations
Insufficient information about minimum balance requirements, account opening procedures, or eligibility criteria
best_for: Faith-aligned consumers seeking community banking with values integration
Business owners in specialized sectors (agriculture, cannabis, healthcare, non-profit, tech) needing industry-specific banking
Borrowers seeking SBA loans and commercial lending with community bank relationship banking
Customers preferring personal relationship banking over large national institutions
diagnosis: Regent Bank is best suited for community-oriented consumers and business owners—particularly those in specialized sectors like agriculture, cannabis, healthcare, or non-profit work—who value faith-based values and personalized banking relationships. The main caveat is that their website provides minimal specific product details, pricing, or service area information, requiring direct contact to evaluate whether they serve your location and meet your specific financial needs.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
